title: Depth recurrence: 3 unique layers x 3 loops, 1.589 BPB
author: koushikkethamakka
body:
## Summary

Exploring depth recurrence for parameter-efficient language modeling. Instead of 9 unique transformer blocks, use 3 unique blocks looped 3 times. Same effective depth, 1/3 the unique parameters. The freed parameter budget goes to wider layers.

## Results

30 experiments across widths (512-1152), layers (2-6), recurrence counts (2-6), and head counts (4-16).

| Config | Iters | Shards | BPB | Compressed |
|--------|-------|--------|-----|-----------|
| 3x3 d1024 h8 | 5000 | 10 | 1.589 | 20.4MB* |
| 3x3 d768 h12 | 5000 | 10 | 1.605 | 11.3MB |
| 3x3 d1024 h8 | 600 | 10 | 1.996 | 13.0MB |
| 3x3 d896 h8 | 600 | 10 | 2.010 | 10.5MB |
| Baseline 9-layer d512 | 200 | 1 | 2.303 | 9.3MB |

*Compression issue to resolve - exceeds 16MB limit. Working on QAT.

## Key Findings

- 3 unique layers with 3 recurrences is the optimal shape
- Wider models win with sufficient data (d1024 > d896 > d768)
- More than 3 recurrences shows diminishing returns
- 8 heads works best at d1024, 12 heads at d768

## Status

Work in progress. Next steps: resolve 16MB compression constraint, QAT, test-time compute, scale to 8xH100s.

## Files

- `our/models/` - Recurrent and enhanced model variants (MLX)
- `our/scripts/` - Experiment runners and adaptive sweep
- `our/docs/APPROACH.md` - Full strategy document
- `results/` - All experiment logs, configs, and results
